methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $256K total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Terran Robotics is a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Zach Dwiel and Danny Weddle. The company has raised $256K to date. The relevant peer universe comprises 9 private robotics firms and 1 public comparable (ticker 277810.KQ), including companies such as 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

The valuation analysis relies on a single applicable method: stage-progression analysis. Using the $256K total raised as a value proxy and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement, this method yields an implied valuation of $640K. A peer-median approach was not applicable due to insufficient Seed or adjacent-stage comparables within the robotics sector; while 11 same-sector peers exist, none met the necessary thresholds for statistical reliability.

The triangulated analysis, constrained to one method, supports a valuation range of $544K (low) to $736K (high), with a median of $640K. This narrow range reflects both the limited methodological convergence and the early-stage nature of the company. Key uncertainties include the lack of narrowly comparable Seed-stage peers in robotics and the absence of revenue or product traction data. A fuller valuation would benefit from additional operating metrics or expanded peer refinement as the company progresses.
